title: Intermittent 403 errors for users assigned the newly created 'analyst' role
problem: Users who were recently assigned the new 'analyst' role receive intermittent 403 Forbidden errors across all dashboard pages. The errors appear randomly regardless of navigation pattern (fresh login, idle return, or in-app navigation). Refreshing the page one or more times resolves the error temporarily. Long-tenured users with established roles are not affected.
root_cause_hypothesis: The permissions for the newly created 'analyst' role are not being consistently propagated across all server instances or cache layers. When a request hits an instance/cache that hasn't loaded the new role's permission grants, it returns 403. Refreshing works because subsequent requests may hit a different instance or trigger a cache refresh that recognizes the role. This points to either: (1) a permissions cache that doesn't invalidate properly when new roles are created, or (2) an eventually-consistent permissions store where some replicas lag behind.
reproduction_steps: Create a new role (e.g., 'analyst') in TaskFlow
Assign the new role to a user
Have the user log in and navigate the dashboard
Observe intermittent 403 errors; refresh the page to see them resolve and recur
environment: TaskFlow dashboard, multi-user environment. Affects multiple users assigned the new 'analyst' role. Started approximately 2 days ago, coinciding with the role's creation and assignment.
impact: All users assigned newly created roles are intermittently locked out of the dashboard. This blocks the 'analyst' role rollout and would affect any future new roles. Multiple team members are impacted.
recommended_fix: Investigate the permissions/authorization caching layer. Specifically: (1) Check whether role-permission mappings are cached and how cache invalidation is triggered when new roles are created. (2) If running multiple app instances behind a load balancer, verify that all instances pick up new role definitions consistently. (3) Look for a TTL-based cache on role permissions that may serve stale 'role not found' results. The fix likely involves ensuring new roles trigger a cache invalidation/refresh across all instances, or treating an unknown role as a cache miss that falls through to the authoritative store.
proposed_test_case: Create a new role with dashboard access permissions, assign it to a test user, and immediately verify that the user can load the dashboard 20+ times consecutively without a single 403. Repeat across multiple server instances if applicable.
information_gaps: Whether server-side logs show the 403 correlating with specific instances or cache misses
The exact caching mechanism used for role-permission lookups (in-memory, Redis, DB query cache, etc.)
Whether other newly created roles (not just 'analyst') exhibit the same behavior
